rationale: The cited paper is not just listed in background; it is explicitly invoked in the discussion to explain a central empirical pattern in the citing paper: predicted intentions differ from actual follow-through. The citing authors summarize Alexander et al. (2008) as showing a desirability-versus-feasibility representation gap and state that this is consistent with their own findings. Although the citation is not used as a primary theoretical foundation for hypotheses or methods, it meaningfully informs interpretation and positioning of the contribution.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Alexander et al. (2008) as convergent evidence for an intention-behavior follow-through gap driven by desirability versus feasibility representations. It leverages that result to interpret its own prediction-versus-redemption mismatch and motivate measuring both prospective intentions and actual completion.
